title: picea alcoquiana subsp. reflexa
intro: Picea alcoquiana subsp. reflexa, also known as the Japanese black pine, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to Japan. The medicinal properties of this plant have been used in traditional Japanese medicine, particularly its resin and bark, which are rich in terpenes and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Picea alcoquiana subsp. reflexa include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
